skill.name: marketing-psychology
skill.description: Apply behavioral science and mental models to marketing decisions, prioritized using a psychological leverage and feasibility scoring system.
skill.summary: Prioritize ethical marketing psychology models by behavior, feasibility, leverage, and testability.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown formulas and ordinary marketing psychology prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network behavior, filesystem operations, prompt injection attempts, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Formula Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command findings at lines 74, 76, 78, 106, and 108 are Markdown code fences and score formulas for PLFS, not Ruby or shell execution. No command interpreter, script file, or user-controlled execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside Markdown code fences that display arithmetic formulas. The skill contains only documentation instructions and no executable Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at lines 3 and 128 appear to match ordinary descriptive text about scoring systems and mental models. No hash function, encryption routine, credential handling, or cryptographic API is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain marketing psychology descriptions, not cryptographic implementation. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding at line 132 is ordinary prose stating that source models remain included. No OS probing, environment enumeration, hostname lookup, or system information collection is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is non-executable Markdown content. I found no commands or instructions that gather host, user, network, or environment details.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams often choose persuasion tactics without a clear link to the target behavior. This skill ranks behavioral models by leverage, fit, speed, ethics, and effort so teams can choose practical tests.
actual_capabilities: Defines the target behavior before recommending any psychology model.
Shortlists relevant behavioral science models for a marketing context.
Scores recommendations with the Psychological Leverage and Feasibility Score.
Maps each selected model to page, pricing, CTA, journey, or retention surfaces.
Includes ethical guardrails and test ideas for each recommendation.
limitations: Does not run experiments or measure conversion results directly.
Does not replace legal, accessibility, or compliance review.
Depends on accurate context about audience, journey stage, and current blockers.
Uses a compact model library instead of a complete academic literature review.

use_cases.title: Improve Pricing Page Decisions
use_cases.description: Identify the strongest psychological levers for plan selection, option clarity, and buyer confidence.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Conversion Tests
use_cases.description: Rank candidate persuasion ideas by likely impact, ethical safety, and speed to signal.

use_cases.title: Review Product Messaging
use_cases.description: Connect messaging changes to a specific behavior and choose models that support informed choice.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single marketing surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketing-psychology skill to analyze this page or message. Define the target behavior, list the likely blockers, and recommend up to three ethical psychology models.

prompt_templates.title: Score a Test Idea
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare one proposed change before implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use PLFS to evaluate this marketing idea. Score leverage, context fit, implementation ease, speed to signal, and ethical safety. Explain whether we should test it.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Journey Interventions
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose actions across awareness, consideration, decision, and retention.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this customer journey and identify where psychology can reduce hesitation. Recommend the top five models, with PLFS scores, surfaces, test ideas, and ethical guardrails.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a conversion optimization program.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a psychology-based experiment roadmap for this product. Segment recommendations by journey stage, rank them with PLFS, define hypotheses, and note risks that should block a test.

output_examples.input: Our pricing page has five plans and users often leave before choosing.
output_examples.output: The skill would likely target plan selection, shortlist models such as Paradox of Choice and Risk Reversal, score each one, then suggest tests like reducing tiers or clarifying the recommended plan.

output_examples.input: We want to add urgency messaging to a checkout page.
output_examples.output: The skill would check whether urgency is truthful and reversible, score its ethical safety, and reject false scarcity if risk is higher than expected leverage.

output_examples.input: New users finish onboarding but do not return after week one.
output_examples.output: The skill would focus on retention behavior, compare models such as Endowment Effect and IKEA Effect, and propose tests that build user investment without locking users in.

best_practices: Start with the exact behavior you want to change.
Provide the audience, journey stage, page or flow, and current blocker.
Ask for ethical guardrails and measurable test ideas with every recommendation.
anti_patterns: Asking for a long list of biases without a target behavior.
Using urgency, scarcity, or defaults when the claim is not transparent.
Treating PLFS scores as proof instead of a prioritization aid.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps select and prioritize ethical marketing psychology models for pages, pricing, copy, journeys, and experiments.

faq.question: Does it generate manipulative tactics?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly rejects dark patterns, false scarcity, hidden defaults, and exploitation of vulnerable users.

faq.question: What is PLFS?
faq.answer: PLFS is a scoring method for leverage, context fit, implementation ease, speed to signal, and ethical safety.

faq.question: Can it replace A/B testing?
faq.answer: No. It helps choose stronger hypotheses, but performance still needs measurement with real users.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the target behavior, audience, journey stage, current blocker, and the surface being changed.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
